title: Agentic RAG vs Pipeline RAG
concept: Contrasting a fixed retrieve-then-generate pipeline with a model that dynamically controls its own retrieval.

exercises.prompt: In the previous lesson, we learned about Tool-Calling Retrieval. How does it change the LLM's access to information?

options.text: It lets the model call a search function with custom queries during reasoning.
It forces the user to write manual SQL queries for the LLM.
It removes the need for vector databases entirely.

exercises.explain: Tool-calling retrieval gives the LLM access to a search engine as a tool, allowing the model to decide when and what parameters to pass to the search function.

cards.title: Breaking the Assembly Line
cards.body: Traditional search systems follow a rigid track: search once, read once, and answer. But what happens if the first search doesn't find the right answer? That's where we swap a fixed pipeline for a thinking agent.

cards.title: Pipeline vs. Agentic RAG
cards.body: Pipeline RAG follows a strict, linear 'retrieve-then-generate' sequence. Agentic RAG replaces this assembly line with an active LLM agent that decides when to search, evaluates the results, and loops back if it needs more information.

cards.title: The Vending Machine vs. The Chef
cards.body: Think of a standard pipeline as a vending machine: you press a button, it dispenses one pre-selected snack. Agentic RAG is like a personal chef who tastes the soup, decides it needs salt, goes to the pantry, grabs spices, and checks the taste again until it is perfect.

cards.title: Multi-Hop Research in Action
cards.body: If you ask a Pipeline RAG 'Compare Apple's 2022 and 2023 revenue,' it runs one search and tries its best. An Agentic RAG agent will search for 'Apple 2022 revenue', read it, then dynamically run a second search for 'Apple 2023 revenue' before writing the final comparison.

cards.title: More Data Isn't the Only Answer
cards.body: Many believe that adding more documents to a database makes a pipeline smarter. In reality, a fixed pipeline easily gets overwhelmed by irrelevant documents, whereas an agent can actively filter out the noise by refining its own search queries.

cards.title: Flow Comparison
cards.body: Comparing how data flows through both architectures. A pipeline is a straight highway, while an agent is a roundabout that lets you exit only when you have the right cargo.

left.text: Search -> Retrieve -> Generate. Fixed steps, no turning back.

right.text: Search -> Evaluate -> Search again? -> Generate. Flexible loop.

exercises.prompt: What is the defining characteristic of a traditional Pipeline RAG system?

options.text: It allows the model to search multiple times.
It uses an agent loop to critique its own answers.
It follows a strict, one-way path of retrieving first and then generating.
It does not use vector embeddings.

exercises.explain: Pipeline RAG follows a rigid Retrieve-then-Generate flow with no decision-making loop to evaluate if the retrieved data is actually helpful.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An Agentic RAG system can perform multiple searches sequentially if the first search doesn't answer the user's query.
exercises.explain: Agentic RAG can evaluate the search results and decide to run new searches if the initial information is incomplete or irrelevant.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to contrast the rigid flow with the flexible agentic approach:
exercises.sentence: Unlike an agent that can retry searches, a linear pipeline executes steps in a fixed order without loopbacks.
exercises.explain: A linear pipeline forces a strict sequence of steps, whereas an agentic system can loop, retry, and adapt its behavior dynamically.

exercises.prompt: Which of these logs represents an Agentic RAG system rather than a standard pipeline?

options.text: I searched the database for 'annual report' and found 3 documents. Here is the summary of the first one.
Error: The search query returned no results. Please try again with different keywords.
The search for 'project deadline' returned empty results. I will now search for 'project schedule' instead to find the date.

exercises.explain: Option C demonstrates an LLM evaluating its search results and making a dynamic decision to query again, which is the hallmark of Agentic RAG. Options A and B are static pipeline outputs.

exercises.prompt: You are designing a system to answer complex customer support tickets that often require looking up multiple related policy documents. Why would you choose Agentic RAG over a standard Pipeline RAG for this task?

options.text: A pipeline uses vector search while Agentic RAG only uses SQL database lookups.
A pipeline follows a fixed, linear path, while Agentic RAG dynamically decides when and what to search based on reasoning.
Pipeline RAG is always more accurate because it bypasses the LLM's decision-making process.
Agentic RAG disables the LLM's generator phase to save computation costs.

exercises.explain: Pipeline RAG follows a rigid, one-way sequence of steps regardless of how complex the query is. Agentic RAG uses a reasoning loop to decide whether it needs to search, what to search for, and whether the retrieved information is sufficient to answer the question.
